introSection: Batgirl Batarang, previously known as Boomerang, is a steel shuttle roller coaster located at Six Flags Mexico in Mexico City, Mexico. It was built by Vekoma.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
This was the first Boomerang ordered from Vekoma, but not the first to open. It began operation in 1984 at Rafaela Padilla and was relocated to Six Flags México in 1988. As of 2013, the ride was sponsored by Fanta.

On November 26, 2024, Six Flags Mexico announced that Boomerang would be permanently removed after the weekend on December 1, 2024.

The park later confirmed a day later that Boomerang wouldn't be completely removed, but rather rethemed into Batgirl for the 2025 season.

The ride reopened as Batgirl Batarang in 2025.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: Purple track and green supports, used to be white track with green supports, followed by red track with white supports.

fullText:
Batgirl Batarang, previously known as Boomerang, is a steel shuttle roller coaster located at Six Flags Mexico in Mexico City, Mexico. It was built by Vekoma.

This was the first Boomerang ordered from Vekoma, but not the first to open. It began operation in 1984 at Rafaela Padilla and was relocated to Six Flags México in 1988. As of 2013, the ride was sponsored by Fanta.

On November 26, 2024, Six Flags Mexico announced that Boomerang would be permanently removed after the weekend on December 1, 2024.

The park later confirmed a day later that Boomerang wouldn't be completely removed, but rather rethemed into Batgirl for the 2025 season.

The ride reopened as Batgirl Batarang in 2025.



Purple track and green supports, used to be white track with green supports, followed by red track with white supports.
